codeReview.summary: The README.md is well-structured, accurate, and covers all required topics from the feature description: what lab is, build instructions, run instructions, shell integration (init/install), key commands (cd, clone, worktree, dot shorthand), all five environment variables, and test commands. Every claim was verified against the actual binary output (--help), source code, and AGENTS.md. No inaccuracies were found. The branding consistently uses 'lab' throughout with no stale 'try' references.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill mandates TDD (step 3: Write Tests First) for all features, but docs-only features like this one have no Rust behavior to test. The worker correctly deviated and noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The skill could benefit from an explicit carve-out for documentation-only features where command verification plus the full validator suite is the appropriate validation strategy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations[0]: step '3. Write Tests First (TDD)', whatIDidInstead: 'Made the README-only change directly, then validated the documented behaviors with command spot checks and the full validator suite.'

summary: The README.md passes review. It is a clean 82-line document that accurately documents all aspects required by the feature description: project overview, build/run instructions, shell integration setup (init + install), key commands table (cd, clone, worktree, dot shorthand), environment variables table (LAB_PATH, LAB_PROJECTS, NO_COLOR, LAB_WIDTH, LAB_HEIGHT), and testing instructions (cargo test + spec runner). All documented commands and behaviors were cross-checked against the actual binary and source code. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
